Title: Nuclear pyruvate dehydrogenase complex regulates histone acetylation and transcriptional regulation in the ethylene response

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes the pyruvate dehydrogenase reaction within the PDC, contributing to conversion of pyruvate into acetyl-CoA
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step as catalytic PDC subunit required for ethylene-induced nuclear acetyl-CoA production
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Substrate for histone acetylation supporting ethylene-responsive transcription
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Basal nuclear distribution without ethylene; nuclear accumulation increases with ethylene treatment duration
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Ethylene gas treatment; ACC treatment; dark-grown etiolated seedlings
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Nuclear/cytoplasmic fractionation; co-IP/MS; in vitro pull-down; reciprocal co-IP; BiFC; loss-of-function mutants; higher-order mutants; overexpression; nucleus-targeted fusion; PDH activity assay; isotope tracing with 2,3-13C2-pyruvate; LC-MS measurement of acetyl-CoA; RNA-seq; ChIP-seq/ChIP-qPCR; complementation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes ethylene response by enabling nuclear acetyl-CoA synthesis and downstream histone acetylation/transcription activation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. E1-2 directly interacts with EIN2-C in the nucleus after ethylene treatment. 2. E1-2 shows basal nuclear presence and further accumulates in the nucleus in response to ethylene. 3. e1 single mutants show little phenotype alone, but e1 strongly enhances ethylene insensitivity in e1e2 and e1e2e3 mutants. 4. Nuclear-targeted E1 triggers ethylene hypersensitivity even without ethylene. 5. Nuclear E1 is largely dephosphorylated and enzymatically active, contributing to ethylene-induced acetyl-CoA production for H3K14/H3K23 acetylation.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Higher-order pdc mutants, especially e1e2e3, show growth defects including shortened hypocotyls/roots in dark and reduced root/vegetative growth in light
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq (Col-0 reference data GEO: GSE77396; mutant RNA-seq accession not stated); ChIP-seq (Col-0 reference data GEO: GSE77396); phosphoproteomics PRIDE: PXD040689
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: E1 phosphorylation at Ser292 was detected; dephosphorylated E1 predominates in the nucleus after ethylene treatment, consistent with active PDC.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers the acetyl group within the PDC to coenzyme A during conversion of pyruvate into acetyl-CoA
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Most important subunit genetically for the ethylene response
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supplies acetyl donor for histone acetylation and ethylene-responsive transcription
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S promoter (constitutive) for overexpression constructs; native E2 promoter in complementation construct proE2:gE2-FLAG-GFP
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Not detected in nucleus without ethylene; accumulates in nucleus after ethylene with duration dependence
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Ethylene gas treatment; ACC treatment; dark-grown etiolated seedlings
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: co-IP/MS; in vitro pull-down; reciprocal co-IP; BiFC; CRISPR weak alleles; higher-order mutants; complementation; overexpression; nucleus-targeted fusion; LC-MS measurement of acetyl-CoA; isotope tracing; RNA-seq; histone acetylation immunoblot; ChIP-seq/ChIP-qPCR
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Major positive determinant of ethylene-induced nuclear acetyl-CoA production, histone acetylation, and transcriptional activation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. E2-2 directly interacts with EIN2-C in the nucleus only after ethylene treatment. 2. E2-2 undergoes ethylene-induced nuclear accumulation. 3. Weak e2 mutants display mild ethylene insensitivity, and double/triple mutants with e1/e3 show stronger defects. 4. E2 overexpression enhances ethylene response in the presence of ACC, while nuclear-targeted E2 is sufficient to trigger ethylene hypersensitivity and increased H3K14ac/H3K23ac without ethylene. 5. E2 complementation rescues mutant ethylene and growth phenotypes.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Null homozygotes are lethal; higher-order mutants including e2 combinations show growth defects
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Dihydrolipoamide acetyltransferase, pyruvate dehydrogenase E2 core component
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq (Col-0 reference data GEO: GSE77396; mutant RNA-seq accession not stated); ChIP-seq (Col-0 reference data GEO: GSE77396)
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Genetically the most important PDC subunit in ethylene response; null homozygous mutants were not recovered because of lethality.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reoxidizes dihydrolipoamide within the PDC, enabling continuous conversion of pyruvate into acetyl-CoA
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Required for direct formation of target product by active PDC complex
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Enhancer of E2 function genetically; not established as sole rate-limiting step
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports histone acetylation and ethylene-responsive transcription via active PDC complex
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Not detected in nucleus without ethylene; accumulates in nucleus after ethylene with duration dependence
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Ethylene gas treatment; ACC treatment; dark-grown etiolated seedlings
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: co-IP/MS; in vitro pull-down; reciprocal co-IP; BiFC; T-DNA mutants; higher-order mutants; overexpression; LC-MS measurement of acetyl-CoA; isotope tracing; RNA-seq; histone acetylation assays
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes ethylene response as part of nuclear PDC complex supplying acetyl-CoA for histone acetylation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. E3-2 directly interacts with EIN2-C in the nucleus after ethylene treatment. 2. E3-2 accumulates in the nucleus in response to ethylene. 3. e3 single mutants are weak, but e2e3 and e1e2e3 mutants show enhanced ethylene insensitivity and reduced histone acetylation/transcriptional activation. 4. E3 overexpression enhances ACC response but is insufficient without hormone when not nuclear localized. 5. E3 acts coordinately with E1 and especially E2 in nuclear PDC function.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Higher-order pdc mutants including e3 combinations show growth defects
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq (Col-0 reference data GEO: GSE77396; mutant RNA-seq accession not stated); ChIP-seq (Col-0 reference data GEO: GSE77396)
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Total E3 protein showed slight elevation after prolonged ethylene treatment, unlike E1 and E2 whose total abundance was largely unchanged.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: EIN2 membrane-associated ethylene signaling protein; C-terminal nuclear signaling fragment EIN2-C
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Signal-dependent nuclear translocation and protein interaction-mediated promotion of histone acetylation/transcriptional activation
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Nuclear acetyl-CoA-coupled histone acetylation supporting ethylene-responsive transcription
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: PDC E1-2; PDC E2-2; PDC E3-2; ethylene-responsive target genes marked by H3K14ac and H3K23ac
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Promotes nuclear accumulation/function of PDC and activates ethylene-responsive transcription via increased H3K14ac/H3K23ac
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Essential upstream regulator required for ethylene-induced nuclear PDC accumulation and acetyl-CoA-dependent histone acetylation
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Forms nuclear functional complex/association with PDC subunits E1-2, E2-2, and E3-2
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: PDC E1-2; PDC E2-2; PDC E3-2; EIN3; EIL1
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Ethylene; ACC; dephosphorylation and cleavage generating EIN2-C
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Substrate for histone acetylation enabling ethylene-responsive transcription
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for PDC nuclear translocation/function and for ethylene-induced histone acetylation/transcriptional activation
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. EIN2-C physically interacts with PDC E1/E2/E3 in the nucleus only after ethylene treatment. 2. EIN2 is required for ethylene-induced nuclear accumulation of PDC subunits. 3. PDC overexpression fails to confer ethylene hypersensitivity in ein2-5, showing EIN2 dependence. 4. Loss of PDC partially suppresses constitutively active EIN2S645A, indicating PDC acts downstream of EIN2-C nuclear accumulation to support histone acetylation and transcription.
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S promoter (constitutive) for some overexpression constructs in genetic tests
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Ethylene gas treatment; ACC treatment; dark-grown etiolated seedlings
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: co-IP/MS; in vitro pull-down; reciprocal co-IP; BiFC; epistasis analysis with ein2-5 and EIN2S645A; microscopy; ChIP-qPCR; gene expression assays
Regulation_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: Functions upstream of increased histone H3K14 and H3K23 acetylation at ethylene-responsive genes through recruitment/requirement of nuclear PDC
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: ChIP-seq and RNA-seq context data include GEO: GSE77396
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although central to the mechanism, EIN2 is a hormone signaling regulator rather than a metabolic enzyme; included because it decisively regulates acetyl-CoA-dependent chromatin control in the study.
